response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and systems software engineer with solid low-latency and distributed systems experience, but his background has no meaningful overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep hardware expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator design, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD analysis, and deep neural network workload optimization — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His AI exposure is limited to agentic tooling and MCP frameworks at the application layer, which is categorically different from hardware-level AI accelerator performance work. The seniority mismatch is compounded by a domain mismatch that cannot be bridged by adjacent experience alone.
strengths: Strong low-latency systems background at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests/second demonstrates performance-oriented thinking
Breadth across embedded, systems, and application layers shows adaptability across the software stack
Long tenure with consistent senior/staff-level IC progression indicates technical depth and ownership capability
concerns: Zero hardware architecture experience — no SoC modeling, IP design, GPU microarchitecture, or SIMD/MIMD exposure anywhere in 16+ years of work
No deep learning workload expertise; AI involvement is purely at the tooling/agent orchestration layer, not model training or inference optimization
Principal-level IC roles at this hardware-software intersection typically require graduate-level hardware education or equivalent; candidate holds a CS BS with no hardware focus

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware modeling experience
No DL workload or GPU architecture background
